rationale: The citing paper is about uplift-based targeting in churn interventions, while the cited paper is primarily a customer-based firm valuation model; the notes show overlap mainly on the importance of retention, not an obvious shared method or direct benchmark. The raw evidence only confirms a DOI match in the reference list and provides no in-text usage context. Because the cited work could still be a foundational motivation source on retention’s value, this pair should not be safely dropped from second-stage review.
